The local context states that COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus and started spreading at the end of 2019.
The local context confirms that the virus spreads mainly through close contact and tiny droplets in the air.
The local context mentions that many people experience mild illness or no symptoms, but it can be severe for older adults and those with certain conditions.
The local context states that COVID-19 can lead to serious complications like acute respiratory distress syndrome and blood clots.
The local context confirms that it's possible to get COVID-19 more than once due to fading immunity and virus mutations.
The external evidence confirms that COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2.
The external evidence states that COVID-19 is caused by infection with the SARS-CoV-2 virus.
The external evidence confirms that COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
The external evidence describes COVID-19 as a 'pandemic', referring to its geographical spread.
The external evidence states that the World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020.
The external evidence explains the meaning of 'CO', 'VI', and 'D' in COVID-19.
The local context confirms COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2.
The local context defines the acronym 'COVID'.
The local context states symptoms may appear 2 to 14 days after exposure.
The local context mentions that some infected individuals may be asymptomatic.
The local context describes COVID-19 as a pandemic.
External evidence confirms COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2.
External evidence states COVID-19 started spreading at the end of 2019.
External evidence confirms COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2.
External evidence states COVID-19 can lead to serious complications.
